name: Google Ads Transparency Center: Advertiser Search
summary: Search the Google Ads Transparency Center by advertiser name and enumerate that advertiser's ads (creative id, format, first/last shown dates, preview) into a source-discriminated manifest.
description: Drives the real Ads Transparency Center UI (homepage search box -> advertiser typeahead -> click suggestion) to resolve an advertiser, then reads that advertiser's creatives from the site's own SearchService.SearchCreatives RPC via a same-origin fetch (page xsrf token, region-scoped). Paginates by the response cursor up to a configurable cap. Pure read. Emits a manifest validating against schema/ads-manifest-v1.json with source=google_ads_transparency.

advertiser.description: Advertiser name to search for, e.g. 'Nike' or 'Booking.com'. Typed into the real search box; the top advertiser suggestion is selected.

region.description: Region to scope ads to. v1 supports 'US' (returns an error naming supported regions otherwise). Recorded in the manifest query.

cap.description: Maximum ads to collect. Pagination follows the RPC cursor and stops cleanly at the cap; truncated=true when more were available.

steps.name: Wait for advertiser creatives to load

help.summary: Search Google Ads Transparency Center by advertiser and list that advertiser's ads.

parameters.advertiser: Advertiser name to search (drives the real typeahead).
parameters.cap: Max ads to collect (default 30, hard cap 300).

examples.description: List up to 30 Nike ads in the US
Small page to verify the cap
examples.command: rzn-browser run google_ads_transparency search --param advertiser="Booking.com" --param cap=10

returns: Object: { source, query{advertiser,advertiser_id,region}, cap, count, truncated, total_available, advertiser_name, advertiser_id, ads:[{ id, advertiser_id, advertiser_name, media_type, format_code, first_shown, last_shown, copy, url, preview_url }] }. On failure to resolve, count=0 and an 'error' string is set.
notes: Drives the homepage search box and clicks the top advertiser suggestion (real UI), then reads the site's own SearchService.SearchCreatives RPC via same-origin fetch with the page xsrf token.
media_type is derived from the creative content (image/video/text); format_code is the raw source code.
first_shown/last_shown are ISO dates (yyyy-mm-dd) converted from the RPC's unix timestamps.
No CDP; all JS runs in the page main world.
